documentation: Create a step, but don't start it yet. This is useful for defining plans.

documentation: Start a step

methods.name: nora_lib.interactions.step_progress.StepProgressReporter.finish
documentation: Finish a step whether it was successful or not

methods.name: nora_lib.interactions.step_progress.StepProgressReporter.create_child_step
documentation: Create a child step

methods.name: nora_lib.interactions.step_progress.StepProgressReporter._save_progress_to_istore
documentation: Save a step progress to the Interactions Store. Returns the event id if successful.

Wrapper around StepProgress to add event metadata and report to interactions service

Usage:
# Create/define a step
find_papers_progress = StepProgressReporter(
    actor_id,
    message_id,
    StepProgress(short_desc="Find papers"),
    interactions_service
)
find_papers_progress.create()

# Do something
...

# Start the step
find_papers_progress.start()

# Do more things
...
count_citation_progress = find_papers_progress.create_child_step(short_desc="Count citations")
count_citation_progress.create()
...
count_citation_progress.start()
...
count_citation_progress.finish(is_success=True)
...

# Finish the step
find_papers_progress.finish(is_success=False, error_message="Something went wrong")

# Alternatively, you can use this as a context. The step will be automatically created.

with StepProgressReporter(...) as spr:
    # Do something
    ...

    # You still need to start the step
    spr.start()

# This step will be automatically finished when the context exits.
# If an exception is raised, the step will be marked as failed
# and the exception message will be recorded in the error_message field

classes.name: nora_lib.interactions.models.CostDetail
documentation:
Base class to store details of cost to service a request by an agent.
If an agent has different cost details,
it should create another class inheriting this class and add those fields.
See LLMCost and LLMTokenBreakdown below for examples.

modules.name: nora_lib.tasks.state
documentation:
Handles IO for asynchronous task-related state.

A StateManager instance must be initialized with
a concrete subclass of `AsyncTaskState`, as implemented
by dependent projects.

classes.name: nora_lib.tasks.state.StateManager
documentation: Stores task state on local disk

methods.name: nora_lib.pubsub.PubsubService.subscribe_webhook
documentation:
Add a webhook subscriber to a topic
The webhook will receive a POST request whenever any client calls publish() on the topic

methods.name: nora_lib.pubsub.PubsubService.unsubscribe_webhook
documentation: Remove a webhook subscriber from a topic

methods.name: nora_lib.pubsub.PubsubService.subscribe_sse
documentation:
Subscribe to a topic using Server-Sent Events
Returns an iterator that yields message payloads as they are published
Will close the underlying HTTP connection when the context manager exits

Usage:

# This will run indefinitely
with pubsub_service.subscribe_sse("my_topic") as messages:
     for message in messages:
            handle(message)

# This will run in the background, exiting after one minute
with pubsub_service.subscribe_sse("my_topic") as messages:
    def run():
        for message in messages:
            handle(message)
    threading.Thread(target=run).start()

    sleep(60)

methods.name: nora_lib.pubsub.PubsubService.publish
documentation: Publish a message to a topic
Client for Nora pubsub backend

methods.name: nora_lib.interactions.interactions_service.InteractionsService.get_virtual_thread_content
documentation:
Fetch all messages and events in a virtual thread
Returns all messages and events in the same thread as the given message,
but filtered to only include those associated with the given virtual thread.
\:param message_id: The ID of a message in the virtual thread
\:param virtual_thread_id: The ID of the virtual thread
